title: ptelea baldwinii subsp. crenulata
intro: Ptelea baldwinii subsp. crenulata, also known as Fremont's prickleback or California prickleback, is a plant species in the Rutaceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Ptelea baldwinii subsp. crenulata include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
